findings.person_name: Thomas Tho T. Adams JS DNA w\CH; BR; VM
findings.search_query: Thomas Adams York Maine 1650 Hannah Parker wife genealogy
findings.source_title: Thomas Adams (1648-1737) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Thomas Adams married Hannah Parker, daughter of John Parker, in York about 1675. Hannah was born 1658 in York, daughter of John and Edith Parker. His will was dated April 5, 1726, mentioning wife and children Hannah, Philip, Samuel, Hezekiah, Hester, Elizabeth, Thomas and Nathan. Born c.1648 York, died 1737.
findings.claimed_facts: Thomas Adams b. c.1648 York, Maine; d. 1737 York; m. Hannah Parker (b.1658, dau. of John & Edith Parker) c.1675; will dated 5 Apr 1726. Son of Phillip Adams.
findings.notes: Garbled worklist name ('Tho T. Adams JS DNA w\CH; BR; VM') resolves to Thomas Adams of York, Maine. Worklist b.1650-1659 and d.abt 1726-1737 are consistent (will 1726, death 1737). Web-tree (WikiTree/Geni) match; not a primary record. DUPLICATE: @I322625937295@ is the same person with identical data.

findings.person_name: Thomas Tho T. Adams JS DNA w\CH; BR; VM
findings.search_query: Thomas Adams York Maine 1650 Hannah Parker wife genealogy
findings.source_title: Thomas Adams (1648-1737) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Thomas Adams married Hannah Parker, daughter of John Parker, in York about 1675. Hannah born 1658 York, daughter of John and Edith Parker. Will dated April 5, 1726.
findings.claimed_facts: Same as @I322625937248@: Thomas Adams b. c.1648 York, Maine; d. 1737; m. Hannah Parker.
findings.notes: DUPLICATE of @I322625937248@ (identical name, dates, spouse). Likely a merge candidate in the tree. Same WikiTree match applies.

findings.search_query: Timothy Matchett Gloucester Massachusetts Susanna Fuller / Susanna Matchett Anthony Day
findings.source_title: Susanna Day (Matchett) (1624 - 1717) - Genealogy (Geni)
findings.snippet: Susanna Matchett was born 1623 in England, arrived in Massachusetts Bay Colony as a servant of William Vinson, and married Anthony Day. (No record located for a male 'Timothy Matchett' b.1600 d.10 Dec 1717 of Gloucester.)
findings.claimed_facts: No source found for a person named Timothy Matchett (male) b.1600 Gloucestershire, d.1717 Gloucester MA. The surname Matchett appears in Gloucester records via Susanna Matchett (b.1623, m. Anthony Day, d.1717).
findings.notes: PROBLEM RECORD / likely garbled or conflated. Worklist name 'Timothy ring Matchett' with spouse 'Susanna Ring Fuller' and death '10 Dec 1717 Gloucester' appears to mix the Matchett surname (Susanna Matchett of Gloucester, d.1717) with the Ring/Fuller family. The stated parents 'Rev. Mitchell Matchett; Maria Shelley' could not be verified. Recommend human review; the death date 1717 may belong to Susanna Matchett, not a 'Timothy.'

findings.search_query: Isaac Mixer Watertown Massachusetts died 1716 Sarah Thurston genealogy
findings.source_title: Isaac Mixer (abt.1630-abt.1716) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Isaac Mixer, b. about 1630, d. about 22 Nov 1716 at Sudbury (also recorded Waltham), Middlesex, MA. Parents: Isaacke Mixer and Sarah (Thurston) Mixer. Married (1) Mary Coolidge 1655, (2) Rebecca Garfield 1661, (3) Mary Lathrop 1687. Emigrated 1634 at age 4 with parents. Sources: NEHGR vol.63 p.277; Watertown Records; Early New England Families 1641-1700.
findings.claimed_facts: Isaac Mixer b. c.1630 (emigrated 1634 from Ipswich England), d. c.22 Nov 1716 Sudbury/Waltham MA; parents Isaacke Mixer & Sarah Thurston; wives Mary Coolidge, Rebecca Garfield, Mary Lathrop.
findings.notes: Strong match on death year (1716), death place (Massachusetts) and parents (worklist lists 'Isaac Tobias MIXER; Sarah THURSTON' = Isaacke Mixer + Sarah Thurston). CONFLICT: worklist spouse 'Rebeckah Newton' does NOT match documented wives (Coolidge/Garfield/Lathrop). Possible spouse-name error in the tree. WikiTree cites primary-derived sources (NEHGR), so confidence is moderate-high on the person, low on the spouse.

findings.search_query: Abraham York North Yarmouth Maine 1682 Mary Richards son of John York
findings.source_title: York Family / Old Kittery and Her Families (Stackpole)
findings.source_site: archive.org / byrnefamily.net (Old Kittery and Her Families)
findings.snippet: Richard York made a will in 1672 bequeathing to sons John, Samuel and Benjamin... The York family of Maine descends through these lines; Samuel York built a garrison at North Yarmouth by 1686.
findings.claimed_facts: York family established in York County / North Yarmouth, Maine in late 1600s; John York was a son of Richard York. Specific record for Abraham York b.1682 m. Mary Richards not directly confirmed.
findings.notes: Could not directly confirm Abraham/Abram York b.1682 North Yarmouth, d.1711, wife Mary Richards, son of John York. The broader York family (Richard York's sons John, Samuel, Benjamin) is well documented in Old Kittery and Her Families, consistent with the claimed parent 'John York.' Needs targeted record search. DUPLICATE: @I322625936208@ is identical.

findings.search_query: Abraham York North Yarmouth Maine 1682 Mary Richards son of John York
findings.source_title: York Family / Old Kittery and Her Families (Stackpole)
findings.snippet: Richard York will 1672 names sons John, Samuel, Benjamin; York family in York County and North Yarmouth, Maine.
findings.claimed_facts: Same as @I322625935848@; Abraham York b.1682 not directly confirmed.
findings.notes: DUPLICATE of @I322625935848@ (identical data). Merge candidate. No direct confirming record located.

findings.search_query: Alice Smalley Truro Massachusetts 1727 wife Samuel Cash
findings.source_title: Samuel Cash (1723-) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Alice (Small/Smalley) Cash born February 17, 1727 in Truro, Barnstable, MA. Married Samuel Cash (b.1723 Provincetown) on March 28, 1742/3. 'Samll Cash of Province Town married to Ailce Smalley of Truroe march 28: 1742/3' (Rev. John Avery), Truro vital records; also Provincetown records. Sources: Bowman, Vital Records of Truro (1933); Records of Provincetown 1698-1859 (NEHGS).
findings.claimed_facts: Alice/Elsie Smalley (Small) b. 17 Feb 1727 Truro, Barnstable, MA; m. Samuel Cash 28 Mar 1742/3. Parents not identified on this page.
findings.notes: Strong match: exact birth date 17 Feb 1727 Truro and spouse Samuel Cash confirmed in two town vital-record sources cited by WikiTree. Worklist 'death aft 1761' not on page but plausible. Maiden name variant Small/Smalley; parents unidentified, so the '?' in the worklist name reflects an open parentage question.

findings.search_query: Ann Allen Kittery Maine 1690 married John Ball Hannah White
findings.source_title: TWIGS and TREES: John BALL, c.1635-1717, Kittery, Maine / Hannah (White) Allen | WikiTree
findings.snippet: John Ball Jr. married Anne Allen on 9 Dec 1717 in Kittery, York, ME. Hannah White (b. abt 1647 Kittery, dau. of John White) married Robert Allen abt 1670 in Kittery; their son Francis Allen had children incl. an Anna who married.
findings.claimed_facts: Anne Allen m. John Ball Jr. 9 Dec 1717, Kittery, York, Maine. Hannah White (b.~1647) m. Robert Allen ~1670 Kittery — Hannah White is an Allen ancestor (likely grandmother).
findings.notes: Marriage of Anne Allen to John Ball (Jr.) in Kittery 1717 confirmed; consistent with worklist (spouse John Ball, Kittery). Worklist 'parent: Hannah White' likely indicates descent from Robert Allen & Hannah White line, but Anne's exact parentage (daughter of Francis Allen?) is not explicitly confirmed. Birth 1690 not directly sourced.

findings.search_query: Anna Beal Parsons York Maine 1657 daughter Arthur Beal Elizabeth Watts
findings.source_title: Arthur Beal (abt.1620-abt.1682) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Arthur Beal Sr. baptized 1638 in Plympton St. Maurice near Plymouth, England; born about 1620; arrived in York (Maine) about 1655. (Specific record for daughter Anna Beal who married a Parsons not displayed.)
findings.claimed_facts: Arthur Beal Sr. (claimed father) b. c.1620, of York, Maine, d. c.1682 — a documented person. Anna Beal b.1657 York, m. into Parsons family of York, not directly confirmed.
findings.notes: Garbled name 'Anna Beale Parsons 11' (trailing '11' is noise). The claimed father Arthur (Thomas) Beal Sr. of York is a real documented settler; mother Elizabeth Watts not confirmed in results. The Parsons family of York, Maine is documented (NH Historical Society 'Parsons genealogy: John and Elizabeth Parsons of York, Maine'). Direct record tying Anna Beal -> Parsons not found; needs human review.

findings.search_query: Anne Carter 1677 Boston married William Cotton Portsmouth New Hampshire
findings.source_title: Anne Cotton (Carter) (1677 - d.) - Genealogy (Geni)
findings.snippet: Anne Carter was born in 1677 in Boston, Massachusetts, to Ralph Carter and Susanna Carter. William (a gunsmith) married Anne in Boston on November 6, 1699; associated with Portsmouth, NH. Children incl. Mary, Elizabeth, Sarah (Treadwell), Ann.
findings.claimed_facts: Anne Carter b.1677 Boston, MA; parents Ralph & Susanna Carter; m. William Cotton (gunsmith) 6 Nov 1699 Boston; family connected to Portsmouth, NH.
findings.notes: Birth (1677 Boston) and spouse (William Cotton) match worklist exactly; Portsmouth NH association matches worklist death place. Death 7 Sep 1770 Portsmouth not confirmed in results. Adds parents Ralph & Susanna Carter (worklist blank). Web-tree (Geni) match. Cotton family of Portsmouth, NH has a published genealogy (archive.org cottonfamilyofpo00cott).

findings.search_query: Benjamin Bickford 1672 Dover New Hampshire son John Bickford Temperance Hull
findings.source_title: Benjamin Bickford (1672-bef.1725) | WikiTree / Genealogy of John Bickford of Durham NH
findings.snippet: Benjamin Bickford, son of John Bickford of Oyster River & Temperance Hull, born October 20, 1672 in Dover, NH; died June 2, 1725 in Newington, NH; married Sarah Barsham (1676-1752), daughter of John and Mehitable Barsham. Constable; living at Bloody Point.
findings.claimed_facts: Benjamin Bickford b. 20 Oct 1672 Dover, NH; d. 2 Jun 1725 Newington, NH; son of John 'English' Bickford & Temperance Hull; m. Sarah Barsham; was a constable.
findings.notes: Exact match on birth date (20 Oct 1672), death date (2 Jun 1725) and death place (Newington), and parents (John Bickford & Temperance Hull) per worklist. NOTE: sources flag conflicting data about two Benjamin Bickfords (the one who m. Sarah Barsham vs. one 'of Bloody Point' with will 1724/25) - human review advised. Worklist spouse blank; documented wife Sarah Barsham.

findings.search_query: Benjamin Browne Hampton New Hampshire 1647 genealogy
findings.source_title: Benjamin Brown (1647-abt.1736) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Benjamin Brown born about 1647 in Hampton, NH, son of John Brown. Died 1 Oct 1735 (some sources ~1736) in Hampton Falls, NH. Married Sarah Brown (dau. of William & Elizabeth Murford Brown of Salisbury) in 1679. Signer of Weare's Petition 1683; selectman 1696-1705, 1711; Representative 1697.
findings.claimed_facts: Benjamin Brown b. c.1647 Hampton, NH; d. 1735/1736 Hampton (Falls), NH; son of John Brown (& Sarah Walker); m. Sarah Brown 1679; selectman/representative.
findings.notes: Strong match: birth ~1647 Hampton, death ~1736 Hampton, spouse Sarah Brown, all match worklist. Worklist parent 'Sarah Ann Walker' = his mother Sarah Walker (wife of John Brown). Some descendant sources differ on death year (1735 vs 1736) and place (Hampton vs Hampton Falls vs Rye).

findings.search_query: Bethiah Mason 1665 Rehoboth Massachusetts married John Wood
findings.source_title: Bethia Mason Wood (1665-unknown) - Find A Grave Memorial
findings.snippet: Bethiah Mason born 15 October 1665 in Rehoboth, MA; married John Wood (married 23 May 1688). Father Sampson Mason (bp. 6 Dec 1624 Bolton le Moor, Lancashire, England), who married Mary Butterworth; removed to Rehoboth.
findings.claimed_facts: Bethiah Mason b. 15 Oct 1665 Rehoboth, MA; m. John Wood 23 May 1688; daughter of Sampson Mason & Mary Butterworth.
findings.notes: Exact birth date (15 Oct 1665 Rehoboth) and spouse (John Wood) match worklist. Worklist parent 'Mary Butterworth Mason' = mother Mary Butterworth (wife of Sampson Mason). Worklist death 27 Aug 1741 Rehoboth not confirmed (Find a Grave shows death unknown). Marriage 1688 newly found.

findings.search_query: Colonel Timothy Gerrish 1684 Kittery Maine Sarah Elliot son John Gerrish
findings.source_title: Colonel Timothy Gerrish (1684-1755) - FamilySearch
findings.snippet: Col. Timothy Gerrish, son of Capt. John and Elizabeth (Waldron) Gerrish, born Dover, NH, April 2, 1684. Married Nov 14, 1706, Sarah, daughter of Hon. Robert Elliot and Sarah (Fryer) Elliot of Newcastle, NH. Settled on Gerrish's Island, Kittery; died Nov 19, 1755. Councillor for Massachusetts and Colonel of the West Yorkshire Regiment. (Per Gerrish Family Genealogy 1617-1917.)
findings.claimed_facts: Col. Timothy Gerrish b. 2/21 Apr 1684 Dover, NH; d. 19 Nov 1755 Gerrish Island, Kittery, ME; son of John Gerrish & Elizabeth Waldron; m. Sarah Elliot 14 Nov 1706. Wealthy merchant, councillor, colonel.
findings.notes: Excellent match across all fields (birth 1684 Dover, death 19 Nov 1755 Gerrish Island Kittery, parents John Gerrish & Elizabeth Waldron, spouse Sarah Elliot). Backed by published Gerrish Family Genealogy (archive.org). CROSS-LINK: his son John Gerrish married Margery Jackson, daughter of Dr. George Jackson & Joanna Pepperrell - both also in this worklist (@I322616879471@, @I322616879434@).

findings.search_query: Thomas Tibbetts 1659 Dover New Hampshire Judith Dam captain
findings.source_title: TIBBETTS, Captain Thomas (1660-1748) - Among Our Ancestors / WeRelate Person:Thomas Tibbetts (2)
findings.snippet: Capt. Thomas Tibbetts born Dover Neck Feb 24, 1659, died there 1748 at age 89. Married Judith Dam July 6, 1684 in Dover Neck (Judith b. Nov 16, 1666, d. Oct 22, 1728). Served as captain in King William's War and Queen Anne's War. Son of Jeremiah Tibbetts, grandson of Henry Tibbetts.
findings.claimed_facts: Capt. Thomas Tibbetts b. 24 Feb 1659/1660 Dover Neck, NH; d. 13 Jul 1748 Dover; m. Judith Dam 6 Jul 1684; son of Jeremiah Tibbetts. Military captain.
findings.notes: Strong match: birth 24 Feb 1659 Dover Neck, death 1748 Dover, spouse Judith Dam, captain - all match worklist. Death date 13 Jul 1748 confirmed by amongourancestors. Note: this Thomas (son of Jeremiah) is distinct from Henry Tibbetts entries (@I322741257943@, @I322625937820@) elsewhere in worklist, who are also Jeremiah's descendants.

findings.search_query: Christopher Noble Portsmouth New Hampshire 1680 Lydia Jackson
findings.source_title: David-H-Hendren-Randolph User Tree / NH Genealogical Record
findings.snippet: Christopher Noble born around 1680 in Portsmouth, NH, son of Christopher Noble and Martha Peverly. Married Lydia Jackson before December 11, 1705. Lydia (b. bef.1690, d. aft.1734) was daughter of John Jackson and Margaret Clark.
findings.claimed_facts: Christopher Noble b. c.1680 Portsmouth, NH; d. 26 Dec 1744 Portsmouth; son of Christopher Noble (II) & Martha Peverly; m. Lydia Jackson before 11 Dec 1705.
findings.notes: Matches worklist: birth ~1680 Portsmouth, parents Christopher Noble & Martha Peverly, spouse Lydia Jackson. Worklist parent 'Martha (Noble) Peverly' = his mother Martha Peverly. Death date 26 Dec 1744 not directly confirmed in results. Source is a user-submitted tree; lower confidence.

findings.search_query: Clement Meserve 1678 Portsmouth New Hampshire Scarborough Maine
findings.source_title: Clement Meserve II (abt.1678-abt.1740) | WikiTree / Empty Branches blog
findings.snippet: Clement Meserve II, son of immigrant Clement I, born Portsmouth NH about 1678. Lived Newington NH on father's homestead until 1726 when he bought 100 acres in Scarborough, Maine (more in 1729). A carpenter. Married (1) Elizabeth Jones 24 Sep 1702 Portsmouth, (2) Sarah Jenkins 14 Aug 1738 Scarborough. Will probated Nov 5, 1746.
findings.claimed_facts: Clement Meservey (Meserve) II b. c.1678 Portsmouth, NH; d. 1746 (will probated 5 Nov 1746) Scarborough, ME; son of Clement Meserve I; carpenter; m. Elizabeth Jones 1702, then Sarah Jenkins 1738.
findings.notes: Strong match: birth ~1678 Portsmouth, moved to Scarborough Maine, death 1746 (worklist '17 Oct 1746 Scarborough') - all consistent. Parents (Clement Jean Meservey & Elizabeth) match. Worklist spouse blank; documented wives Elizabeth Jones & Sarah Jenkins. Backed by FamilySearch and detailed blog research.

findings.search_query: Cornelius Darling 1674 Braintree Massachusetts Bellingham Mary Frebray
findings.source_title: The Darling Family in America (Weeks) / Dennis Darling of Braintree & Mendon (Geni)
findings.snippet: Cornelius Darling, son of Dennis Darling of Braintree, born March 25, 1675 (also given Jan 25, 1675), died April 1763. Brothers John and Cornelius were both weavers and farmers. John Darling was among those setting off Bellingham from Mendon in 1719, connecting the Darlings to Bellingham, MA.
findings.claimed_facts: Cornelius Darling b. Mar 1675 (worklist 23 Mar 1674) Braintree, MA; son of Dennis Darling; weaver/farmer; family associated with Bellingham, MA.
findings.notes: Person matches (Cornelius Darling, son of Dennis Darling of Braintree, Bellingham connection). CONFLICTS: worklist birth 23 Mar 1674 vs sources Mar 25 1675; worklist death 1741 vs source 'April 1763' (the death date discrepancy is significant - possible two records conflated, or the worklist's 1741 is erroneous). Spouse 'Mary Frebray' NOT confirmed in any source - human review advised.

findings.search_query: John Flanders 1659 Salisbury Massachusetts corporal son Stephen Flanders
findings.source_title: John Flanders (1659-1716) | WikiTree FREE Family Tree
findings.snippet: Corporal John Flanders born 11 Feb 1659 in Salisbury, Essex, MA to Stephen Flanders and Jane (Sandusky) Flanders. Died 24 Dec 1716 Salisbury. Married Elizabeth Sargent ('so called') Jan 18, 1688. On 25 Nov 1702 he served as corporal in the scouts under Capt. Henry True Jr. against the Indians around Salisbury.
findings.claimed_facts: Corp. John Flanders b. 11 Feb 1659 Salisbury, MA; d. 24 Dec 1716 Salisbury; son of Stephen Flanders & Jane; m. Elizabeth Sargent 18 Jan 1688; served as corporal in colonial militia 1702.
findings.notes: Excellent match: birth 11 Feb 1659 Salisbury, death 24 Dec 1716 Salisbury, corporal rank, father Stephen Flanders - all match worklist. CAVEAT: worklist mother 'Jane Indian Princess Sandusky' - the 'Sandusky' surname and 'Indian Princess' attribution are widely circulated but considered dubious/folkloric in genealogical scholarship; the source repeats 'Jane Sandusky' but the romanticized label should be flagged for human review.

findings.search_query: Dominicus Jordan 1715 Cape Elizabeth Maine Phebe Gray
findings.source_title: Phebe Jordan (Gray) (c.1715-1777) / Maj. Dominicus Jordan Jr. (Geni)
findings.snippet: Dominicus born June 15, 1715, died 1786; married Phebe Gray. Born in Spurwink, Cape Elizabeth, Cumberland, Maine; died 1786 Cumberland, Maine. Son of (Maj.) Dominicus Jordan Jr. (b.1683) and Hannah Tristram. Phebe married Dominicus Jordan 21 Feb 1740 in Falmouth; children Joanna, Sarah, Arabella (m. Meserve) Jordan.
findings.claimed_facts: Dominicus Jordan b. 15 Jun 1715 Cape Elizabeth (Spurwink), ME; d. 1786 Cape Elizabeth/Cumberland, ME; m. Phebe Gray 21 Feb 1740 Falmouth; son of Dominicus Jordan Jr. & Hannah Tristram.
findings.notes: Strong match: birth 15 Jun 1715 Cape Elizabeth, death 1786 Cape Elizabeth, spouse Phebe Gray, father Dominicus Jordan Jr. - all match worklist ('John/Dominicus Jordan Jr. Captain'). Phebe Gray's own parentage is disputed in sources (John Gray & Susanna Clarke vs George Gray & Mary Murriner). Web-tree match; Jordan family also documented in Genealogical and Family History of Maine.
